prompt: A regional report endpoint failed a staged authorization rollout. A tenant whose overlay intentionally contains no allowed regions still inherits the platform region list, and principals carrying an endpoint read scope can reach the report without all tenant-wide controls. Another trace shows the inverse: tenant-wide controls alone can satisfy an endpoint that is supposed to require one of its report scopes. Explicit non-empty region overlays work, blocked regions remain blocked, and principals with the full tenant controls plus either accepted endpoint scope are healthy. Config deserialization preserves the distinction between an omitted property and a present empty array. Keep the decision response shape and service API stable, preserve case-sensitive scope names, avoid hard-coding tenant or route names, and edit only candidate sources.
